concerns.text: SC-005 states: 'requiring [deferred] of group comparisons use associational language'. The placeholder '[deferred]' is not a measurable threshold. Success criteria must define a verifiable condition (e.g., '100% of interpretation paragraphs must lack causal verbs like 'causes' or 'leads to''). A placeholder prevents a pass/fail test.

US-2 AS1 requires 'statistically valid group-level effect estimates' but does not define the minimum sample size or power threshold required to deem the test 'valid' in this specific context. While US-4 mentions ≥10 participants, US-2's acceptance criteria lack a concrete, measurable definition of 'statistically valid' (e.g., 'power ≥ 0.8' or 'n ≥ 20 per group') to allow a deterministic pass/fail check.

FR-002 mandates preprocessing 'completing within ≤4 hours per dataset'. The spec does not define the 'dataset' size (e.g., number of participants or volume) against which this 4-hour limit is measured. Without a defined dataset scope (e.g., 'per dataset of up to 20 participants'), the requirement is unverifiable as the time constraint scales with data volume.
